title: convolvulus subauriculatus
intro: Convolvulus subauriculatus, also known as the small-flowered bindweed or morning glory, is a perennial herb in the Convolvulaceae family, native to tropical Africa and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and fever. Convolvulus subauriculatus has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some traditional medicine practices, it is used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for diarrhea.
